mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 3.8%, wage growth accelerates and consumer spending expands, driving corporate revenue growth and pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 3.8%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with strong nonfarm payrolls indicate robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and lifts equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity multiples over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns for two consecutive years, or the signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, real rates turn deeply negative, compressing bond yields and forcing capital into equities, which drives short-term S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: Equity valuations contract when real rates are negative, or the combined inflation-funds rate signal yields a negative information coefficient over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms secular economic expansion, which systematically raises corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth and equity returns diverge for three consecutive quarters, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000 per month alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightness, which boosts consumer-driven corporate revenues and lifts equity prices over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The labor market signal fails to predict positive equity returns in more than 65% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real personal savings exceeding 5% indicates consumer deleveraging and spending contraction, which reduces corporate revenue growth and pushes S&P 500 prices lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Consumer savings drawdowns do not correlate with equity underperformance, or the signal produces positive forward returns in over 60% of triggered periods.
